title: San Francisco Sourdough Boule

seo.title: How to Make San Francisco Sourdough Boule — Easy American Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic San Francisco Sourdough Boule. This easy American recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make San Francisco Sourdough Boule
easy San Francisco Sourdough Boule recipe
authentic American San Francisco Sourdough Boule
lowCompetitionKeywords: American San Francisco Sourdough Boule recipe
seo.h1: Authentic San Francisco Sourdough Boule Recipe
h2Headings: What is San Francisco Sourdough Boule?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic American San Francisco Sourdough Boule recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade San Francisco Sourdough Boule — a beloved American classic

description: San Francisco Sourdough Boule is a true testament to American baking, renowned for its distinct tangy flavor and chewy interior. This iconic bread boasts a wonderfully crisp crust, often adorned with a beautiful scoring pattern. Perfect for sandwiches, toast, or simply served with butter, it embodies the spirit of traditional San Francisco baking.

instructions.title: Mix Ingredients
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the active sourdough starter, lukewarm water, and fine sea salt. Stir until the salt is dissolved and the starter is mostly incorporated. Add the bread flour and mix by hand until no dry spots remain and a shaggy dough forms. Cover the bowl and let it rest for 30 minutes. This is known as the autolyse phase.

instructions.title: Bulk Fermentation and Stretch & Folds
instructions.text: After the rest, perform your first set of stretch and folds. Grab a portion of the dough, stretch it upwards, and fold it over itself. Rotate the bowl and repeat this process 3-4 times. Cover the dough and let it rest for 30 minutes. Repeat the stretch and fold process three more times, with 30-minute rests in between each set. After the last fold, let the dough bulk ferment at room temperature for 3-4 hours or until it has increased in volume by about 30-50 percent. This is mostly inactive time.

instructions.title: Preshape and Rest
instructions.text: Lightly flour your work surface. Gently turn the dough out onto the surface. Carefully preshape the dough into a loose round using a bench scraper or your hands, being careful not to degas it too much. Allow the preshaped dough to rest uncovered on the counter for 20-30 minutes. This helps the gluten relax before the final shaping.

instructions.title: Final Shape and Cold Proof
instructions.text: After the rest, lightly flour the top of the preshaped dough. Flip it over and perform your final shaping, creating a taut boule. Place the shaped dough seam-side up into a floured banneton or a bowl lined with a floured kitchen towel. Cover the banneton tightly with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 8-12 hours, or overnight, for a cold proof. This develops flavor and makes the dough easier to handle.

instructions.title: Preheat and Bake
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 450°F (232°C) with a Dutch oven inside for at least 30 minutes. Carefully remove the hot Dutch oven from the oven. Gently invert the cold dough from the banneton into the hot Dutch oven. Score the top of the dough with a sharp razor blade or knife. Cover the Dutch oven and bake for 30 minutes.

instructions.title: Finish Baking and Cool
instructions.text: After 30 minutes, remove the lid from the Dutch oven. Continue to bake the bread uncovered for another 15-20 minutes, or until the crust is deeply golden brown and crispy. The internal temperature should reach around 200-210°F (93-99°C). Carefully remove the boule from the Dutch oven and transfer it to a wire rack to cool completely for at least 1-2 hours before slicing. Slicing too early can result in a gummy interior.

tips: Ensure your sourdough starter is very active and bubbly before beginning, as this is crucial for a good rise and open crumb structure.
For the best crust, always bake sourdough in a preheated Dutch oven or a similar lidded heavy pot. The steam trapped inside creates that signature crispy crust.

Always allow your freshly baked sourdough boule to cool completely on a wire rack for at least an hour or two before slicing. This allows the internal moisture to redistribute, preventing a gummy texture.
article: San Francisco Sourdough Boule is a beloved American Baking dish enjoyed across American. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
